hypothesis: Augmenting uMLIP training data with defect-containing and non-equilibrium MOF structures—created by introducing common defects (e.g., missing linkers, under-coordinated metal centers) and sampling high-temperature MD perturbations—will improve zero-shot transferability to unseen MOFs with open metal sites and diverse porosities, reducing MD instability events and structural-optimization volume errors on MOFSimBench compared with models trained solely on pristine equilibrium data.
